findings.description: Main document title block has lost its centered alignment and visual prominence.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the header block containing 'FACT SHEET', 'DRAFT PERMIT RENEWAL', and facility details is centered and uses the standard body font size, acting as the document's main title. In the HTML, this block is rendered as small, grey, left-aligned text, significantly changing its visual hierarchy.

findings.description: Loss of indentation for contact information block.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the contact details block starting with 'Mr. Adam Ulishney, Chief...' is indented to visually separate it from the surrounding paragraphs. In the HTML, this block is fully left-aligned.

findings.description: Page artifacts interrupt the logical reading flow mid-sentence.
findings.evidence: A sentence is split across the page break in the PDF: '...Persons wishing to' (page 1) 'comment on the proposed...' (page 2). The HTML inserts the page 1 footer and the page 2 header block directly between these two parts of the sentence, disrupting the continuous reading order.

findings.description: Hyperlink is present as plain text but lacks an active HTML anchor (<a>) tag.
findings.evidence: PDF contains an active link for 'https://edocs.deq.nc.gov/WasteManagement/Welcome.aspx'. The HTML renders this as plain text: 'online at https://edocs.deq.nc.gov/WasteManagement/Welcome.aspx using the EPA ID'.

findings.description: The HTML document skips the <h1> level in its heading hierarchy.
findings.evidence:
The document contains

tags (e.g., '

Background

') but does not contain an

tag for the document title.
